repair_analysis.what_to_keep: intent to reduce approach region dominance and incentivize landing
repair_analysis.what_to_modify: less aggressive terminal bonus increase, paired with stronger intermediate stability/landing-quality guidance
repair_analysis.what_to_remove: overly aggressive terminal multiplier (factor 5.0) and de-emphasized intermediate guidance
repair_analysis.why_repaired_plan_is_safer: The new plan increases the terminal bonus only modestly (factor 2.5) and simultaneously boosts stability and landing-quality rewards, ensuring the agent receives guidance to achieve stable contact before the large terminal reward becomes the sole target. This avoids the risk of sparse terminal pressure on an agent with no stability evidence.

diagnosis: Agent exploits dense approach shaping; rebalance with cautious terminal increase and stronger intermediate guidance to prevent instability.

reward_editor.rationale: The repair reduces the previously flagged terminal pressure by using a lower multiplier (2.5) and increases weights on stability and landing quality to guide stable landing. This provides a smoother incentive shift, preserving gradient while encouraging safe landing.
